verses.latin_vulgate: Respiciens autem, vidit eos qui mittebant munera sua in gazophylacium, divites.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναβλέψας δὲ εἶδεν τοὺς βάλλοντας τὰ δῶρα αὐτῶν εἰς τὸ γαζοφυλάκιον πλουσίους
verses.king_james: And he looked up, and saw the rich men casting their gifts into the treasury.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidit autem et quamdam viduam pauperculam mittentem æra minuta duo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶδεν δέ καὶ τινα χήραν πενιχρὰν βάλλουσαν ἐκεῖ δύο λεπτὰ
verses.king_james: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit: Vere dico vobis, quia vidua hæc pauper plus quam omnes misit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ἀληθῶς λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἡ χήρα ἡ πτωχὴ αὕτη πλεῖον πάντων ἔβαλεν
verses.king_james: And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam omnes hi ex abundanti sibi miserunt in munera Dei: hæc autem ex eo quod deest illi, omnem victum suum quem habuit, misit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἅπαντες γὰρ οὗτοι ἐκ τοῦ περισσεύοντος αὐτοῖς ἔβαλον εἰς τὰ δῶρα τοῦ θεοῦ, αὕτη δὲ ἐκ τοῦ ὑστερήματος αὐτῆς ἅπαντα τὸν βίον ὃν εἶχεν ἔβαλεν
verses.king_james: For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quibusdam dicentibus de templo quod bonis lapidibus et donis ornatum esset, dixit:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί τινων λεγόντων περὶ τοῦ ἱεροῦ ὅτι λίθοις καλοῖς καὶ ἀναθήμασιν κεκόσμηται εἶπεν
verses.king_james: And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts, he said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc quæ videtis, venient dies in quibus non relinquetur lapis super lapidem, qui non destruatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα ἃ θεωρεῖτε ἐλεύσονται ἡμέραι ἐν αἷς οὐκ ἀφεθήσεται λίθος ἐπὶ λίθῳ ὃς οὐ καταλυθήσεται
verses.king_james: *As for* these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrogaverunt autem illum, dicentes: Præceptor, quando hæc erunt, et quod signum cum fieri incipient?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπηρώτησαν δὲ αὐτὸν λέγοντες διδάσκαλε πότε οὖν ταῦτα ἔσται καὶ τί τὸ σημεῖον ὅταν μέλλῃ ταῦτα γίνεσθαι
verses.king_james: And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what sign *will there be* when these things shall come to pass?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit: Videte ne seducamini: multi enim venient in nomine meo, dicentes quia ego sum: et tempus appropinquavit: nolite ergo ire post eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν βλέπετε μὴ πλανηθῆτε πολλοὶ γὰρ ἐλεύσονται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου λέγοντες ὃτι ἐγώ εἰμι καί ὁ καιρὸς ἤγγικεν μὴ οὖν πορευθῆτε ὀπίσω αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And he said, Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am *Christ*; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem audieritis prælia et seditiones, nolite terreri: oportet primum hæc fieri, sed nondum statim finis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἀκούσητε πολέμους καὶ ἀκαταστασίας μὴ πτοηθῆτε δεῖ γὰρ ταῦτα γενέσθαι πρῶτον ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ εὐθέως τὸ τέλος
verses.king_james: But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end *is* not by and by.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicebat illis: Surget gens contra gentem, et regnum adversus regnum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς ἐγερθήσεται ἔθνος ἐπὶ ἔθνος καὶ βασιλεία ἐπὶ βασιλείαν
verses.king_james: Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et terræmotus magni erunt per loca, et pestilentiæ, et fames, terroresque de cælo, et signa magna erunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σεισμοί τε μεγάλοι κατὰ τόπους καὶ λιμοὶ καὶ λοιμοὶ ἔσονται φόβητρά τε καὶ σημεῖα ἀπ᾽ οὐρανοῦ μεγάλα ἔσται
verses.king_james: And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ante hæc omnia injicient vobis manus suas, et persequentur tradentes in synagogas et custodias, trahentes ad reges et præsides propter nomen meum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸ δὲ τούτων ἅπαντων ἐπιβαλοῦσιν ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῶν καὶ διώξουσιν παραδιδόντες εἰς συναγωγὰς καὶ φυλακάς ἀγομένους ἐπὶ βασιλεῖς καὶ ἡγεμόνας ἕνεκεν τοῦ ὀνόματός μου
verses.king_james: But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute *you*, delivering *you* up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name's sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: continget autem vobis in testimonium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποβήσεται δὲ ὑμῖν εἰς μαρτύριον
verses.king_james: And it shall turn to you for a testimony.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ponite ergo in cordibus vestris non præmeditari quemadmodum respondeatis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θέσθε οὖν εἴς τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν μὴ προμελετᾶν ἀπολογηθῆναι
verses.king_james: Settle *it* therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before what ye shall answer:

verses.latin_vulgate: ego enim dabo vobis os et sapientiam, cui non poterunt resistere et contradicere omnes adversarii vestri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ γὰρ δώσω ὑμῖν στόμα καὶ σοφίαν ᾗ οὐ δυνήσονται ἀντειπεῖν οὐδὲ ἀντιστῆναι πάντες οἱ ἀντικείμενοι ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist.

verses.latin_vulgate: Trademini autem a parentibus, et fratribus, et cognatis, et amicis, et morte afficient ex vobis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραδοθήσεσθε δὲ καὶ ὑπὸ γονέων καὶ ἀδελφῶν καὶ συγγενῶν καὶ φίλων καὶ θανατώσουσιν ἐξ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and *some* of you shall they cause to be put to death.

verses.latin_vulgate: et eritis odio omnibus propter nomen meum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσεσθε μισούμενοι ὑπὸ πάντων διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου
verses.king_james: And ye shall be hated of all *men* for my name's sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: et capillus de capite vestro non peribit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ θρὶξ ἐκ τῆς κεφαλῆς ὑμῶν οὐ μὴ ἀπόληται
verses.king_james: But there shall not an hair of your head perish.

verses.latin_vulgate: In patientia vestra possidebitis animas vestras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ ὑπομονῇ ὑμῶν κτήσασθε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: In your patience possess ye your souls.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem videritis circumdari ab exercitu Jerusalem, tunc scitote quia appropinquavit desolatio ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἴδητε κυκλουμένην ὑπὸ στρατοπέδων τήν ἰερουσαλήμ τότε γνῶτε ὅτι ἤγγικεν ἡ ἐρήμωσις αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that the desolation thereof is nigh.

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc qui in Judæa sunt, fugiant ad montes, et qui in medio ejus, discedant: et qui in regionibus, non intrent in eam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οἱ ἐν τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ φευγέτωσαν εἰς τὰ ὄρη καὶ οἱ ἐν μέσῳ αὐτῆς ἐκχωρείτωσαν καὶ οἱ ἐν ταῖς χώραις μὴ εἰσερχέσθωσαν εἰς αὐτήν
verses.king_james: Then let them which are in Judaea flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the countries enter thereinto.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia dies ultionis hi sunt, ut impleantur omnia quæ scripta sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἡμέραι ἐκδικήσεως αὗταί εἰσιν τοῦ πληρωθῆναι πάντα τὰ γεγραμμένα
verses.king_james: For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ autem prægnantibus et nutrientibus in illis diebus! erit enim pressura magna super terram, et ira populo huic.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ δὲ ταῖς ἐν γαστρὶ ἐχούσαις καὶ ταῖς θηλαζούσαις ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις ἔσται γὰρ ἀνάγκη μεγάλη ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς καὶ ὀργὴ ἐν τῷ λαῷ τούτῳ
verses.king_james: But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cadent in ore gladii, et captivi ducentur in omnes gentes, et Jerusalem calcabitur a gentibus, donec impleantur tempora nationum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πεσοῦνται στόματι μαχαίρας καὶ αἰχμαλωτισθήσονται εἰς πάντα τὰ ἔθνη καὶ ἰερουσαλὴμ ἔσται πατουμένη ὑπὸ ἐθνῶν ἄχρι πληρωθῶσιν καιροὶ ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erunt signa in sole, et luna, et stellis, et in terris pressura gentium præ confusione sonitus maris, et fluctuum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσται σημεῖα ἐν ἡλίῳ καὶ σελήνῃ καὶ ἄστροις καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς συνοχὴ ἐθνῶν ἐν ἀπορίᾳ ἠχούσης θαλάσσης καὶ σάλου
verses.king_james: And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;

verses.latin_vulgate: arescentibus hominibus præ timore, et exspectatione, quæ supervenient universo orbi: nam virtutes cælorum movebuntur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποψυχόντων ἀνθρώπων ἀπὸ φόβου καὶ προσδοκίας τῶν ἐπερχομένων τῇ οἰκουμένῃ αἱ γὰρ δυνάμεις τῶν οὐρανῶν σαλευθήσονται
verses.king_james: Men's hearts failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be shaken.

verses.latin_vulgate: et tunc videbunt Filium hominis venientem in nube cum potestate magna et majestate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τότε ὄψονται τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενον ἐν νεφέλῃ μετὰ δυνάμεως καὶ δόξης πολλῆς
verses.king_james: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: His autem fieri incipientibus, respicite, et levate capita vestra: quoniam appropinquat redemptio vestra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀρχομένων δὲ τούτων γίνεσθαι ἀνακύψατε καὶ ἐπάρατε τὰς κεφαλὰς ὑμῶν διότι ἐγγίζει ἡ ἀπολύτρωσις ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit illis similitudinem: Videte ficulneam, et omnes arbores:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν παραβολὴν αὐτοῖς Ἴδετε τὴν συκῆν καὶ πάντα τὰ δένδρα
verses.king_james: And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;

verses.latin_vulgate: cum producunt jam ex se fructum, scitis quoniam prope est æstas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν προβάλωσιν ἤδη βλέποντες ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτῶν γινώσκετε ὅτι ἤδη ἐγγὺς τὸ θέρος ἐστίν
verses.king_james: When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ita et vos cum videritis hæc fieri, scitote quoniam prope est regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ὅταν ἴδητε ταῦτα γινόμενα γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγύς ἐστιν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen dico vobis, quia non præteribit generatio hæc, donec omnia fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ παρέλθῃ ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη ἕως ἂν πάντα γένηται
verses.king_james: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cælum et terra transibunt: verba autem mea non transibunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ παρελεύσονται οἱ δὲ λόγοι μου οὐ μὴ παρέλθωσιν
verses.king_james: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite autem vobis, ne forte graventur corda vestra in crapula, et ebrietate, et curis hujus vitæ, et superveniat in vos repentina dies illa:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέχετε δὲ ἑαυτοῖς μήποτε βαρυνθῶσιν ὑμῶν αἱ καρδίαι ἐν κραιπάλῃ καὶ μέθῃ καὶ μερίμναις βιωτικαῖς καὶ αἰφνίδιος ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς ἐπιστῇ ἡ ἡμέρα ἐκείνη
verses.king_james: And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and *so* that day come upon you unawares.

verses.latin_vulgate: tamquam laqueus enim superveniet in omnes qui sedent super faciem omnis terræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς παγίς γὰρ ἐπελεύσεται ἐπὶ πάντας τοὺς καθημένους ἐπὶ πρόσωπον πάσης τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vigilate itaque, omni tempore orantes, ut digni habeamini fugere ista omnia quæ futura sunt, et stare ante Filium hominis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγρυπνεῖτε οὖν ἐν παντὶ καιρῷ δεόμενοι ἵνα καταξιωθῆτε ἐκφυγεῖν ταῦτα πάντα τὰ μέλλοντα γίνεσθαι καὶ σταθῆναι ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem diebus docens in templo: noctibus vero exiens, morabatur in monte qui vocatur Oliveti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ τὰς ἡμέρας ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ διδάσκων τὰς δὲ νύκτας ἐξερχόμενος ηὐλίζετο εἰς τὸ ὄρος τὸ καλούμενον ἐλαιῶν
verses.king_james: And in the day time he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called *the mount* of Olives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnis populus manicabat ad eum in templo audire eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὁ λαὸς ὤρθριζεν πρὸς αὐτὸν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἀκούειν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.
